matches.moment: Mokoena's corner-kick header levels the game at 1-1 at 64'; David converts a penalty at 79' as South Africa push numbers forward and leave space behind.

verdict: Sunday in Los Angeles opens the round of 32 with a fixture that puts football's expanded format on full display: a historic first for both nations, and a moment that, by any pre-tournament projection, neither was expected to reach. The numbers are unambiguous about who the better team is — Canada lead all 32 remaining qualifiers in xG differential at +1.77 per match, and Jonathan David has established himself as the tournament's most dangerous finisher with three goals in three appearances. What the numbers also reveal, however, is that South Africa are not simply making up the numbers. Their adjusted xGA of 1.01 against comparable opposition matches Canada's almost identically, their goalkeeper Ronwen Williams is among the elite in this tournament, and the absence of Zwane — the red-card ghost from matchday one against Mexico — has been absorbed across two further group performances without structural collapse. Canada's path to victory runs directly through breaking down a compact defensive block that has allowed only two goals across three group games, and doing so with significant uncertainty around their primary midfield anchor, Eustaquio. The most dangerous scenario for Canada is not South Africa winning convincingly — it is a 1-1 draw at 90 minutes that takes this tie to extra time and then to Ronwen Williams guarding the penalty spot. Canada win this in normal time, but the margin is narrow, the scoreline is contested, and the tournament's first knockout day ends not with the comprehensive statement result Canada's xG data suggests they deserve, but with the kind of hard-fought 2-1 that establishes a co-host's knockout credentials without fully settling the questions about their resilience under maximum pressure. — The Analyst.

players.name: Jonathan David
players.stat: 3 goals (hat trick vs Qatar); highest xG rate among active strikers in tournament
players.watch: Second goal of the match — David has scored multiple times in two of three WC appearances; penalty-area movement is his primary weapon

players.name: Ronwen Williams
players.stat: 13 club clean sheets this season; Ballon d'Or top-10 GK (2024); tourney xGA 3.43 but only 2 goals conceded
players.watch: One-on-one reactions and penalty stops — South Africa advance only if Williams performs at peak level

players.name: Teboho Mokoena
players.stat: 1 WC goal (83' penalty vs Czechia); set-piece authority and aerial threat
players.watch: Corners and free kicks — South Africa's most probable route to an equalizer; fills Zwane's creative void

players.name: Tajon Buchanan
players.stat: 1 WC assist; direct dribbling pace on the left channel
players.watch: First 20 minutes — will attempt to establish dominance vs Mudau and force South Africa into a deep, passive block

players.name: Oswin Appollis
players.stat: 1 WC goal (vs South Korea); counter-attack leader with high pace
players.watch: Transition moments after Canada lose the ball high — primary South Africa counter-attacking threat with Mofokeng
